name: Hillary Clinton
subject: My message to Muslim Americans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a campaign supported by hundreds of thousands of grassroots donors
body:
We must stand together. | Friend --

Donald Trump has made a name for himself in this election by trafficking in prejudice and paranoia. Now he says he wants to stop all Muslims from entering the United States. That's a shameful idea. It's also dangerous. At a time when America should be doing everything we can to fight radical jihadists, Mr. Trump is supplying them with new propaganda. He's playing right into their hands.

Some Republicans have been quick to condemn Trump's statements, but the truth is, many GOP candidates have also said extreme things about Muslims. Ben Carson says a Muslim shouldn't be president, Marco Rubio compares Muslims to members of the Nazi party, and Jeb Bush and Ted Cruz have suggested we should only allow Syrian refugees to come to America if they're Christian.

**[I’m proud that you and I are working to build a country that stays true to our founding principles, where all people are welcome to practice their faith free of persecution. If you're with me, I hope you'll add your name right now.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=33&ms=MjQyMDMxMTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzAxMDA0NjA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

To Muslim Americans, I want to say this: What you're hearing from Trump and other Republicans is absolutely, unequivocally wrong. I'm proud to be your fellow American, and many, many other Americans feel the same way. Now is the time for all of us to stand up to hateful, dangerous words and deeds.

Just a few days ago, two young women wearing headscarves were trying to have breakfast at a café in Austin when another diner started shouting racist things at them. The girls left in tears -- in part because of the ugly words, but also because no one came to their defense.

As they left, one of the girls asked the room, "Who cares about us?” Somebody called out, "Nobody." "We left," they said, “because it was true."

But it's not true. And we have to make sure Muslim Americans know that. It's how we stay true to our values as Americans. And it's how we show the world who we really are -- a strong, proud, united country that still knows, after all these years, that all men and women are created equal.

**If you agree, I hope you'll add your name to stand with me right now:**
| [ | [**Add my name**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=16&ms=MjQyMDMxMTMS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzAxMDA0NjA1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Thank you,

Hillary

| Paid for by Hillary for America, a campaign supported by hundreds of thousands of grassroots donors.

name: Lindsey Graham
subject: Not "Ultimately" -- NOW
body:
LindseyGraham.com

Friend, not ultimately. Now. Will you help?

Friend,

In the wake of last week's ISIS-inspired terrorist attack in San
Bernardino, President Obama gave an address from the Oval Office
insisting he has a strategy to destroy ISIS. But what Americans
see instead is an incremental, reactive, indirect approach that
assumes time is on our side. It is not.

What's needed is a strategy to destroy ISIS - not "ultimately,"
as the president said last year, but as quickly as possible.

Earlier this week I co-authored an op-ed in The Wall Street
Journal with Senator John McCain, in which we laid out precisely
what needs to be done to destroy ISIS and end the threat it poses
to the Middle East and the West. Here are just a few highlights:

At the current pace, Islamic State will still control Mosul
and Raqqa, the Syrian city that ISIS claims as its capital, at
the end of next year. Meanwhile, ISIS is metastasizing across the
region, to Libya especially.

After more than a year of an indecisive military campaign, the
U.S. still does not have the initiative. The threat is growing
and evolving faster than the administration's efforts to counter
it. What's needed is a comprehensive civil-military strategy to
destroy ISIS quickly, while creating conditions that can prevent
it, or a threat like it, from ever re-emerging. In short, America
must not only win the war, but also prepare to win the peace. The
U.S. has repeatedly failed to do this, and cannot afford to yet
again.

President Obama is fond of invoking lessons from America's recent
wars. The simplest and most important lesson, however, is the one
he rarely mentions: Apocalyptic terrorists cannot be allowed to
have sanctuary in ungoverned spaces, from which to plan attacks
against the West. Over the past seven years, those conditions
have grown across the Middle East and Africa. If these threats
are not removed now, and quickly, no one should be surprised when
America gets attacked again.

Friend, ISIS must be destroyed as quickly as possible - and I'm
the only candidate running for President who has a real plan to
do exactly that, which is why I'm urgently asking for your
support today.

Our campaign has set a 24-hour deadline to raise $10,000 from
supporters who will help me make my case to the American people
and succeed Barack Obama as Commander-in-Chief.

Please take this immediate opportunity to make a generous
contribution of $25, $50, $75, $100 or even more right now before
our urgent 24 hour deadline.

If you stand with me, please let me hear from you today.

Sincerely,

Lindsey Graham

P.S. Friend, America needs a President who has a plan to defeat
ISIS as quickly as possible and has the determination and
experience to end this barbaric threat to America and our allies.
I know I can be that President with your support. Will you make a
contribution of at least $25 before our urgent 24-hour deadline?
Thank you.

Quick Donate Here >>>

Paid for by Lindsey
Graham 2016

name: Rand Paul
subject: Now we know this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
These past few weeks have been a huge wake-up call for Americans. Not just because of the attacks in Paris and San Bernardino, but also because it revealed a lot about my fellow Republican presidential candidates and their tendency to favor big government over individual liberty. We have Donald Trump shouting from the rooftops that he's in favor of shutting down mosques in the United States. Perhaps in an attempt to one-up that statement, Rubio went a step further to say _" it's not about closing down mosques. It's about closing down any place -- whether it's a cafe, a diner, an internet site -- any place where radicals are being inspired."_ I'm not even sure how to appropriately respond to that. **Now, in addition to listening to every phone call and collecting every email, we should also shut down businesses where someone might be "inspired"? ** Are we forgetting that these policies affect innocent Americans? We cannot be sacrificing liberty for a false sense of safety. | | [If you agree please commit to a generous contribution TODAY!](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32170525:W4SJWvyNx:m:1:879035888:7460CCBF537595E03F57C035FCD41414:r)
---
On top of those ridiculous statement coming from men who hope to lead this country, Marco Rubio proposed a permanent extension of the NSA's ineffective and unconstitutional mass surveillance program.

Donald Trump, Chris Christie, and Jeb Bush? They support it too. And they haven't denounced the idea of a "database" for every American.

They want government list of all citizens and their interests. I guess having a record of all your private messages wouldn't be enough for these guys.

These are the kinds of [BS policies](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32170526:W4SJWvyNx:m:1:879035888:7460CCBF537595E03F57C035FCD41414:r) we need to keep fighting.

**We should not be throwing out the values of the constitution because we are afraid.**

Shutting down places of worship, keeping databases of citizens, collecting all private data -- that should make us afraid. It sets a dangerous precedent that puts every American at risk. Can you imagine an anti-Christian president using those same powers that Trump, Rubio and Carson want to use?

Would you be comfortable with that? I wouldn't at all, and that's the point.
We are bound to the Constitution for a reason. We cannot allow fear to trick us into giving away our freedom, or anyone else's.
**[Will you stand with me today?](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32170525:W4SJWvyNx:m:1:879035888:7460CCBF537595E03F57C035FCD41414:r)**
In liberty,
Rand Paul

name: Marco Rubio
subject: This hits close to home
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45323349:JFD4MSLN7:m:1:1349778030:B74F56EBCAF8A93C86B2F87A45977676:r)
---
| | | | Peter, This morning in Iowa, my brother Mario joined me for the first time on the campaign trail, to discuss an issue I care deeply about but that he knows better than I do: how we treat our veterans. Mario served in the U.S. Army in the late 1960s, and injuries and health issues he developed during his service have led him into various snafus with the VA. He knows personally how our federal government hasn't lived up to the promises we make to those who've put their lives on the line to protect us. Vets like Mario aren't getting the support and care they've been promised and that they've worked so hard to get. The VA scandals under the Obama Administration were just an alarm bell about how an outdated system is failing too many veterans. As a senator, I've fought for VA reform and gotten results, and as President, I'd make it a top priority. In my talk today, I laid out three principles for fixing how we serve veterans: The first is **accountability**. In the Senate, I've repeatedly pushed for measures to make it easier for the VA to fire incompetent, corrupt or negligent employees who aren't meeting the needs of our veterans. This commonsense idea has met resistance from powerful government unions, but we've already met some progress: The VA bill passed last year provided for the first time authority to fire senior VA officials who are failing on the job. _Without accountability, vets are never going to get the care they need and deserve._ The second is **transparency**. It would be great if I could cite how many incompetent or corrupt employees the VA has been able to fire because of the reform I pushed in 2014, but _we actually have no idea_. Only if the VA is going to be properly transparent will we able to fully address the terrible failures that have been revealed over the past couple years. As President, I'd require the department to report publicly on all of its operations, including quality, safety, patient experience, timeliness, and cost-effectiveness. The third principle is **choice**. The VA is never going to be able to provide all the services veterans need, where and when they need them, and it's never going to provide top-notch care if there isn't some element of competition. I want to preserve traditional VA services, but vets need to have the option to go elsewhere to get care, too. The idea should be that _benefits follow the veteran, rather than the veteran having to follow the benefits._ [**Go here to read the speech I gave today, which has more about my plans for the VA.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45323350:JFD4MSLN7:m:1:1349778030:B74F56EBCAF8A93C86B2F87A45977676:r) A president's first responsibility is to be Commander-in-Chief, and that means honoring our military not just when our troops are on active duty or abroad -- it means honoring the sacrifice they've made for years following. As President, I'd follow through on that commitment, with the principles and specific priorities I've outlined. I owe it to my brother, and the millions of others who've served. Thanks for reading, and for your support,
Marco Rubio
---

---

body:
I need you with me. | Friend --

We are just three weeks away from 2016, and now more than ever, I need to know you’re with me.

This campaign won’t be won or lost based on who receives the most checks from billionaires. It’s about whose team will take that volunteer shift, help a neighbor register to vote, or chip in $1 to help us cross the finish line strong.

**[I think we’ve got the best team in this race. If you’re with me, will you chip in $1 right now to say you’re a part of it?](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjQyMTE4NjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=NzAxMDgxOTAxS0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Your donation is about much more than helping us hit our fundraising goals before the year ends. It’s about declaring proudly that we’re in this together, and we’re ready to do what it takes to win.

Hillary

name: Zack Exley, Bernie 2016
subject: Help us take down Trump
body:
#
Dear Friend,
Poll after poll shows that Bernie Sanders is the candidate best suited to take on Donald Trump and every other Republican running for president.
We need to show the best of America -- not the worst that Trump exemplifies. The way we do that is to help Bernie win.
There's an opportunity to get out in your local community and volunteer to make Bernie's vision for America -- not Trump's fascist dystopia -- a reality.
Here's the information for the event:
**Host: **Fellow Bernie Supporter in Silver Spring
**What:** Silver Spring Happy Hour Phonebank Party
**Where:** Blair House
8201 16th Street Apt. 921
Silver Spring, MD 20910
**When:** Saturday, December 12th
4:00 pm EST
**[Yes, I'll be there.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/event/detail/4vdrk)**
**[Click here to RSVP»](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/event/detail/4vdrk)**
Donald Trump's call to ban all Muslims from coming to the United States isn't just deeply offensive, it's dangerous. Especially as it empowers his supporters to act out against Muslims, Latinos, and African Americans.
The United States is a great nation when we stand together, but we are weak when we allow racism and xenophobia to divide us. So let's send a message that we stand together by getting out and volunteering in our community for a presidential candidate who will deliver on his promises to stand up for all of us.
Please join fellow Bernie supporters Saturday at Silver Spring Happy Hour Phonebank Party in Silver Spring.
You can **[click here for more details and to RSVP.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/event/detail/4vdrk)** If you can't make it to this event, **[check our map](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb291d/43f17890/4c66ce7b/62178ae0/169731959/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyMDkwMiJ9/ "http://map.berniesanders.com/#zipcode=20902&distance=50&eventtype=CW&eventtype=E&eventtype=D&eventtype=B&daterange=all-events&sort=distance")** for other phone banking parties and find more events near you.
In solidarity,
Zack Exley
Bernie 2016

** **
** **
** **
Paid for by Bernie 2016
PO Box 905 - Burlington VT 05402 United States - (855) 4-BERNIE
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb291d/43f17890/4c66ce7b/62178ae1/169731959/VEsE/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/change-your-information?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb291d/43f17890/4c66ce7b/62178aee/169731959/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIwOTAyIn0=/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb291d/43f17890/4c66ce7b/62178aef/169731959/VEsC/ "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Stand against the powerful special interests who are systematically buying our Congress and have their sights set on the presidency by [contributing to Bernie here](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb291d/43f17890/4c66ce7b/62178aec/169731959/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/lets-go-bernie?refcode=email_footer").
